    98→// New returns an authenticator.Request or an error that supports the standard
    99→// Kubernetes authentication mechanisms.
   100→func (config Config) New(serverLifecycle context.Context) (authenticator.Request, func(context.Context, *apiserver.AuthenticationConfiguration) error, *spec.SecurityDefinitions, spec3.SecuritySchemes, error) {
   101→	var authenticators []authenticator.Request
   102→	var tokenAuthenticators []authenticator.Token
   103→	securityDefinitionsV2 := spec.SecurityDefinitions{}
   104→	securitySchemesV3 := spec3.SecuritySchemes{}
   105→
   106→	// front-proxy, BasicAuth methods, local first, then remote
   107→	// Add the front proxy authenticator if requested
   108→	if config.RequestHeaderConfig != nil {
   109→		requestHeaderAuthenticator := headerrequest.NewDynamicVerifyOptionsSecure(
   110→			config.RequestHeaderConfig.CAContentProvider.VerifyOptions,
   111→			config.RequestHeaderConfig.AllowedClientNames,
   112→			config.RequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,
   113→			config.RequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,
   114→			config.RequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,
   115→			config.RequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,
   116→		)
   117→		authenticators = append(authenticators, authenticator.WrapAudienceAgnosticRequest(config.APIAudiences, requestHeaderAuthenticator))
   118→	}
   119→
   120→	// X509 methods
   121→	if config.ClientCAContentProvider != nil {
   122→		certAuth := x509.NewDynamic(config.ClientCAContentProvider.VerifyOptions, x509.CommonNameUserConversion)
   123→		authenticators = append(authenticators, certAuth)
   124→	}
   125→
   126→	// Bearer token methods, local first, then remote
   127→	if len(config.TokenAuthFile) > 0 {
   128→		tokenAuth, err := newAuthenticatorFromTokenFile(config.TokenAuthFile)
   129→		if err != nil {
   130→			return nil, nil, nil, nil, err
   131→		}
   132→		tokenAuthenticators = append(tokenAuthenticators, authenticator.WrapAudienceAgnosticToken(config.APIAudiences, tokenAuth))
   133→	}
   134→	if config.ServiceAccountPublicKeysGetter != nil {
   135→		serviceAccountAuth, err := newLegacyServiceAccountAuthenticator(config.ServiceAccountPublicKeysGetter, config.ServiceAccountLookup, config.APIAudiences, config.ServiceAccountTokenGetter, config.SecretsWriter)
   136→		if err != nil {
   137→			return nil, nil, nil, nil, err
   138→		}
   139→		tokenAuthenticators = append(tokenAuthenticators, serviceAccountAuth)
   140→	}
   141→	if len(config.ServiceAccountIssuers) > 0 && config.ServiceAccountPublicKeysGetter != nil {
   142→		serviceAccountAuth, err := newServiceAccountAuthenticator(config.ServiceAccountIssuers, config.ServiceAccountPublicKeysGetter, config.APIAudiences, config.ServiceAccountTokenGetter)
   143→		if err != nil {
   144→			return nil, nil, nil, nil, err
   145→		}
   146→		tokenAuthenticators = append(tokenAuthenticators, serviceAccountAuth)
   147→	}
   148→
   149→	if config.BootstrapToken && config.BootstrapTokenAuthenticator != nil {
   150→		tokenAuthenticators = append(tokenAuthenticators, authenticator.WrapAudienceAgnosticToken(config.APIAudiences, config.BootstrapTokenAuthenticator))
   151→	}
   152→
   153→	// NOTE(ericchiang): Keep the OpenID Connect after Service Accounts.
   154→	//
   155→	// Because both plugins verify JWTs whichever comes first in the union experiences
   156→	// cache misses for all requests using the other. While the service account plugin
   157→	// simply returns an error, the OpenID Connect plugin may query the provider to
   158→	// update the keys, causing performance hits.
   159→	var updateAuthenticationConfig func(context.Context, *apiserver.AuthenticationConfiguration) error
   160→	if config.AuthenticationConfig != nil {
   161→		initialJWTAuthenticator, err := newJWTAuthenticator(serverLifecycle, config.AuthenticationConfig, config.OIDCSigningAlgs, config.APIAudiences, config.ServiceAccountIssuers)
   162→		if err != nil {
   163→			return nil, nil, nil, nil, err
   164→		}
   165→
   166→		jwtAuthenticatorPtr := &atomic.Pointer[jwtAuthenticatorWithCancel]{}
   167→		jwtAuthenticatorPtr.Store(initialJWTAuthenticator)
   168→
   169→		updateAuthenticationConfig = (&authenticationConfigUpdater{
   170→			serverLifecycle:     serverLifecycle,
   171→			config:              config,
   172→			jwtAuthenticatorPtr: jwtAuthenticatorPtr,
   173→		}).updateAuthenticationConfig
   174→
   175→		tokenAuthenticators = append(tokenAuthenticators,
   176→			authenticator.TokenFunc(func(ctx context.Context, token string) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error) {
   177→				return jwtAuthenticatorPtr.Load().jwtAuthenticator.AuthenticateToken(ctx, token)
   178→			}),
   179→		)
   180→	}
   181→
   182→	if len(config.WebhookTokenAuthnConfigFile) > 0 {
   183→		webhookTokenAuth, err := newWebhookTokenAuthenticator(config)
   184→		if err != nil {
   185→			return nil, nil, nil, nil, err
   186→		}
   187→
   188→		tokenAuthenticators = append(tokenAuthenticators, webhookTokenAuth)
   189→	}
   190→
   191→	if len(tokenAuthenticators) > 0 {
   192→		// Union the token authenticators
   193→		tokenAuth := tokenunion.New(tokenAuthenticators...)
   194→		// Optionally cache authentication results
   195→		if config.TokenSuccessCacheTTL > 0 || config.TokenFailureCacheTTL > 0 {
   196→			tokenAuth = tokencache.New(tokenAuth, true, config.TokenSuccessCacheTTL, config.TokenFailureCacheTTL)
   197→		}
   198→		authenticators = append(authenticators, bearertoken.New(tokenAuth), websocket.NewProtocolAuthenticator(tokenAuth))
   199→
   200→		securityDefinitionsV2["BearerToken"] = &spec.SecurityScheme{
   201→			SecuritySchemeProps: spec.SecuritySchemeProps{
   202→				Type:        "apiKey",
   203→				Name:        "authorization",
   204→				In:          "header",
   205→				Description: "Bearer Token authentication",
   206→			},
   207→		}
   208→		securitySchemesV3["BearerToken"] = &spec3.SecurityScheme{
   209→			SecuritySchemeProps: spec3.SecuritySchemeProps{
   210→				Type:        "apiKey",
   211→				Name:        "authorization",
   212→				In:          "header",
   213→				Description: "Bearer Token authentication",
   214→			},
   215→		}
   216→	}
   217→
   218→	if len(authenticators) == 0 {
   219→		if config.Anonymous.Enabled {
   220→			return anonymous.NewAuthenticator(config.Anonymous.Conditions), nil, &securityDefinitionsV2, securitySchemesV3, nil
   221→		}
   222→		return nil, nil, &securityDefinitionsV2, securitySchemesV3, nil
   223→	}
   224→
   225→	authenticator := union.New(authenticators...)
   226→
   227→	authenticator = group.NewAuthenticatedGroupAdder(authenticator)
   228→
   229→	if config.Anonymous.Enabled {
   230→		// If the authenticator chain returns an error, return an error (don't consider a bad bearer token
   231→		// or invalid username/password combination anonymous).
   232→		authenticator = union.NewFailOnError(authenticator, anonymous.NewAuthenticator(config.Anonymous.Conditions))
   233→	}
   234→
   235→	return authenticator, updateAuthenticationConfig, &securityDefinitionsV2, securitySchemesV3, nil
   236→}
   237→
   238→type jwtAuthenticatorWithCancel struct {
   239→	jwtAuthenticator authenticator.Token
   240→	healthCheck      func() error
   241→	cancel           func()
   242→}
   243→
   244→func newJWTAuthenticator(serverLifecycle context.Context, config *apiserver.AuthenticationConfiguration, oidcSigningAlgs []string, apiAudiences authenticator.Audiences, disallowedIssuers []string) (_ *jwtAuthenticatorWithCancel, buildErr error) {
   245→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(serverLifecycle)
   246→
   247→	defer func() {
   248→		if buildErr != nil {
   249→			cancel()
   250→		}
   251→	}()
   252→	var jwtAuthenticators []authenticator.Token
   253→	var healthChecks []func() error
   254→	for _, jwtAuthenticator := range config.JWT {
   255→		// TODO remove this CAContentProvider indirection
   256→		var oidcCAContent oidc.CAContentProvider
   257→		if len(jwtAuthenticator.Issuer.CertificateAuthority) > 0 {
   258→			var oidcCAError error
   259→			oidcCAContent, oidcCAError = dynamiccertificates.NewStaticCAContent("oidc-authenticator", []byte(jwtAuthenticator.Issuer.CertificateAuthority))
   260→			if oidcCAError != nil {
   261→				return nil, oidcCAError
   262→			}
   263→		}
   264→		oidcAuth, err := oidc.New(ctx, oidc.Options{
   265→			JWTAuthenticator:     jwtAuthenticator,
   266→			CAContentProvider:    oidcCAContent,
   267→			SupportedSigningAlgs: oidcSigningAlgs,
   268→			DisallowedIssuers:    disallowedIssuers,
   269→		})
   270→		if err != nil {
   271→			return nil, err
   272→		}
   273→		jwtAuthenticators = append(jwtAuthenticators, oidcAuth)
   274→		healthChecks = append(healthChecks, oidcAuth.HealthCheck)
   275→	}
   276→	return &jwtAuthenticatorWithCancel{
   277→		jwtAuthenticator: authenticator.WrapAudienceAgnosticToken(apiAudiences, tokenunion.NewFailOnError(jwtAuthenticators...)), // this handles the empty jwtAuthenticators slice case correctly
   278→		healthCheck: func() error {
   279→			var errs []error
   280→			for _, check := range healthChecks {
   281→				if err := check(); err != nil {
   282→					errs = append(errs, err)
   283→				}
   284→			}
   285→			return utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)
   286→		},
   287→		cancel: cancel,
   288→	}, nil
   289→}
   290→
   291→type authenticationConfigUpdater struct {
   292→	serverLifecycle     context.Context
   293→	config              Config
   294→	jwtAuthenticatorPtr *atomic.Pointer[jwtAuthenticatorWithCancel]
   295→}
   296→
   297→// the input ctx controls the timeout for updateAuthenticationConfig to return, not the lifetime of the constructed authenticators.
   298→func (c *authenticationConfigUpdater) updateAuthenticationConfig(ctx context.Context, authConfig *apiserver.AuthenticationConfiguration) error {
   299→	updatedJWTAuthenticator, err := newJWTAuthenticator(c.serverLifecycle, authConfig, c.config.OIDCSigningAlgs, c.config.APIAudiences, c.config.ServiceAccountIssuers)
   300→	if err != nil {
   301→		return err
   302→	}
   303→
   304→	var lastErr error
   305→	if waitErr := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(ctx, 10*time.Second, true, func(_ context.Context) (done bool, err error) {
   306→		lastErr = updatedJWTAuthenticator.healthCheck()
   307→		return lastErr == nil, nil
   308→	}); lastErr != nil || waitErr != nil {
   309→		updatedJWTAuthenticator.cancel()
   310→		return utilerrors.NewAggregate([]error{lastErr, waitErr}) // filters out nil errors
   311→	}
   312→
   313→	oldJWTAuthenticator := c.jwtAuthenticatorPtr.Swap(updatedJWTAuthenticator)
   314→	go func() {
   315→		t := time.NewTimer(time.Minute)
   316→		defer t.Stop()
   317→		select {
   318→		case <-c.serverLifecycle.Done():
   319→		case <-t.C:
   320→		}
   321→		// TODO maybe track requests so we know when this is safe to do
   322→		oldJWTAuthenticator.cancel()
   323→	}()
   324→
   325→	return nil
   326→}
   327→
   328→// newAuthenticatorFromTokenFile returns an authenticator.Token or an error
   329→func newAuthenticatorFromTokenFile(tokenAuthFile string) (authenticator.Token, error) {
   330→	tokenAuthenticator, err := tokenfile.NewCSV(tokenAuthFile)
   331→	if err != nil {
   332→		return nil, err
   333→	}
   334→
   335→	return tokenAuthenticator, nil
   336→}
   337→
   338→// newLegacyServiceAccountAuthenticator returns an authenticator.Token or an error
   339→func newLegacyServiceAccountAuthenticator(publicKeysGetter serviceaccount.PublicKeysGetter, lookup bool, apiAudiences authenticator.Audiences, serviceAccountGetter serviceaccount.ServiceAccountTokenGetter, secretsWriter typedv1core.SecretsGetter) (authenticator.Token, error) {
   340→	if publicKeysGetter == nil {
   341→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no public key getter provided")
   342→	}
   343→	validator, err := serviceaccount.NewLegacyValidator(lookup, serviceAccountGetter, secretsWriter)
   344→	if err != nil {
   345→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("while creating legacy validator, err: %w", err)
   346→	}
   347→
   348→	tokenAuthenticator := serviceaccount.JWTTokenAuthenticator([]string{serviceaccount.LegacyIssuer}, publicKeysGetter, apiAudiences, validator)
   349→	return tokenAuthenticator, nil
   350→}
   351→
   352→// newServiceAccountAuthenticator returns an authenticator.Token or an error

    37→/*
    38→ * By default, the following metric is defined as falling under
    39→ * ALPHA stability level https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/blob/master/keps/sig-instrumentation/1209-metrics-stability/kubernetes-control-plane-metrics-stability.md#stability-classes)
    40→ *
    41→ * Promoting the stability level of the metric is a responsibility of the component owner, since it
    42→ * involves explicitly acknowledging support for the metric across multiple releases, in accordance with
    43→ * the metric stability policy.
    44→ */
    45→var clientCertificateExpirationHistogram = metrics.NewHistogram(
    46→	&metrics.HistogramOpts{
    47→		Namespace: "apiserver",
    48→		Subsystem: "client",
    49→		Name:      "certificate_expiration_seconds",
    50→		Help:      "Distribution of the remaining lifetime on the certificate used to authenticate a request.",
    51→		Buckets: []float64{
    52→			0,
    53→			1800,     // 30 minutes
    54→			3600,     // 1 hour
    55→			7200,     // 2 hours
    56→			21600,    // 6 hours
    57→			43200,    // 12 hours
    58→			86400,    // 1 day
    59→			172800,   // 2 days
    60→			345600,   // 4 days
    61→			604800,   // 1 week
    62→			2592000,  // 1 month
    63→			7776000,  // 3 months
    64→			15552000, // 6 months
    65→			31104000, // 1 year
    66→		},
    67→		StabilityLevel: metrics.ALPHA,
    68→	},
    69→)
    70→
    71→func init() {
    72→	legacyregistry.MustRegister(clientCertificateExpirationHistogram)
    73→}

    75→// UserConversion defines an interface for extracting user info from a client certificate chain
    76→type UserConversion interface {
    77→	User(chain []*x509.Certificate) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error)
    78→}
    79→
    80→// UserConversionFunc is a function that implements the UserConversion interface.
    81→type UserConversionFunc func(chain []*x509.Certificate) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error)
    82→
    83→// User implements x509.UserConversion
    84→func (f UserConversionFunc) User(chain []*x509.Certificate) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error) {
    85→	return f(chain)
    86→}

    88→func columnSeparatedHex(d []byte) string {
    89→	h := strings.ToUpper(hex.EncodeToString(d))
    90→	var sb strings.Builder
    91→	for i, r := range h {
    92→		sb.WriteRune(r)
    93→		if i%2 == 1 && i != len(h)-1 {
    94→			sb.WriteRune(':')
    95→		}
    96→	}
    97→	return sb.String()
    98→}

   100→func certificateIdentifier(c *x509.Certificate) string {
   101→	return fmt.Sprintf(
   102→		"SN=%d, SKID=%s, AKID=%s",
   103→		c.SerialNumber,
   104→		columnSeparatedHex(c.SubjectKeyId),
   105→		columnSeparatedHex(c.AuthorityKeyId),
   106→	)
   107→}
   108→
   109→// VerifyOptionFunc is function which provides a shallow copy of the VerifyOptions to the authenticator.  This allows
   110→// for cases where the options (particularly the CAs) can change.  If the bool is false, then the returned VerifyOptions
   111→// are ignored and the authenticator will express "no opinion".  This allows a clear signal for cases where a CertPool
   112→// is eventually expected, but not currently present.
   113→type VerifyOptionFunc func() (x509.VerifyOptions, bool)
   114→
   115→// Authenticator implements request.Authenticator by extracting user info from verified client certificates
   116→type Authenticator struct {
   117→	verifyOptionsFn VerifyOptionFunc
   118→	user            UserConversion
   119→}
   120→
   121→// New returns a request.Authenticator that verifies client certificates using the provided
   122→// VerifyOptions, and converts valid certificate chains into user.Info using the provided UserConversion
   123→func New(opts x509.VerifyOptions, user UserConversion) *Authenticator {
   124→	return NewDynamic(StaticVerifierFn(opts), user)
   125→}
   126→
   127→// NewDynamic returns a request.Authenticator that verifies client certificates using the provided
   128→// VerifyOptionFunc (which may be dynamic), and converts valid certificate chains into user.Info using the provided UserConversion
   129→func NewDynamic(verifyOptionsFn VerifyOptionFunc, user UserConversion) *Authenticator {
   130→	return &Authenticator{verifyOptionsFn, user}
   131→}
   132→
   133→// AuthenticateRequest authenticates the request using presented client certificates
   134→func (a *Authenticator) AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error) {
   135→	if req.TLS == nil || len(req.TLS.PeerCertificates) == 0 {
   136→		return nil, false, nil
   137→	}
   138→
   139→	// Use intermediates, if provided
   140→	optsCopy, ok := a.verifyOptionsFn()
   141→	// if there are intentionally no verify options, then we cannot authenticate this request
   142→	if !ok {
   143→		return nil, false, nil
   144→	}
   145→	if optsCopy.Intermediates == nil && len(req.TLS.PeerCertificates) > 1 {
   146→		optsCopy.Intermediates = x509.NewCertPool()
   147→		for _, intermediate := range req.TLS.PeerCertificates[1:] {
   148→			optsCopy.Intermediates.AddCert(intermediate)
   149→		}
   150→	}
   151→
   152→	/*
   153→			kubernetes mutual (2-way) x509 between client and apiserver:
   154→
   155→				1. apiserver sending its apiserver certificate along with its publickey to client
   156→				2. client verifies the apiserver certificate sent against its cluster certificate authority data
   157→				3. client sending its client certificate along with its public key to the apiserver
   158→				>4. apiserver verifies the client certificate sent against its cluster certificate authority data
   159→
   160→		    	description:
   161→					here, with this function,
   162→					client certificate and pub key sent during the handshake process
   163→					are verified by apiserver against its cluster certificate authority data
   164→
   165→				normal args related to this stage:
   166→					--client-ca-file string   If set, any request presenting a client certificate signed by
   167→						one of the authorities in the client-ca-file is authenticated with an identity
   168→						corresponding to the CommonName of the client certificate.
   169→
   170→					(retrievable from "kube-apiserver --help" command)
   171→					(suggested by @deads2k)
   172→
   173→				see also:
   174→					- for the step 1, see: staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/options/serving.go
   175→					- for the step 2, see: staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/transport/transport.go
   176→					- for the step 3, see: staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/transport/transport.go
   177→	*/
   178→
   179→	remaining := req.TLS.PeerCertificates[0].NotAfter.Sub(time.Now())
   180→	clientCertificateExpirationHistogram.WithContext(req.Context()).Observe(remaining.Seconds())
   181→	chains, err := req.TLS.PeerCertificates[0].Verify(optsCopy)
   182→	if err != nil {
   183→		return nil, false, fmt.Errorf(
   184→			"verifying certificate %s failed: %w",
   185→			certificateIdentifier(req.TLS.PeerCertificates[0]),
   186→			err,
   187→		)
   188→	}
   189→
   190→	var errlist []error
   191→	for _, chain := range chains {
   192→		user, ok, err := a.user.User(chain)
   193→		if err != nil {
   194→			errlist = append(errlist, err)
   195→			continue
   196→		}
   197→
   198→		if ok {
   199→			return user, ok, err
   200→		}
   201→	}
   202→	return nil, false, utilerrors.NewAggregate(errlist)
   203→}
   204→
   205→// Verifier implements request.Authenticator by verifying a client cert on the request, then delegating to the wrapped auth
   206→type Verifier struct {
   207→	verifyOptionsFn VerifyOptionFunc
   208→	auth            authenticator.Request
   209→
   210→	// allowedCommonNames contains the common names which a verified certificate is allowed to have.
   211→	// If empty, all verified certificates are allowed.
   212→	allowedCommonNames StringSliceProvider
   213→}
   214→
   215→// NewVerifier create a request.Authenticator by verifying a client cert on the request, then delegating to the wrapped auth
   216→func NewVerifier(opts x509.VerifyOptions, auth authenticator.Request, allowedCommonNames sets.String) authenticator.Request {
   217→	return NewDynamicCAVerifier(StaticVerifierFn(opts), auth, StaticStringSlice(allowedCommonNames.List()))
   218→}
   219→
   220→// NewDynamicCAVerifier create a request.Authenticator by verifying a client cert on the request, then delegating to the wrapped auth
   221→func NewDynamicCAVerifier(verifyOptionsFn VerifyOptionFunc, auth authenticator.Request, allowedCommonNames StringSliceProvider) authenticator.Request {
   222→	return &Verifier{verifyOptionsFn, auth, allowedCommonNames}
   223→}
   224→
   225→// AuthenticateRequest verifies the presented client certificate, then delegates to the wrapped auth
   226→func (a *Verifier) AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error) {
   227→	if req.TLS == nil || len(req.TLS.PeerCertificates) == 0 {
   228→		return nil, false, nil
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	// Use intermediates, if provided
   232→	optsCopy, ok := a.verifyOptionsFn()
   233→	// if there are intentionally no verify options, then we cannot authenticate this request
   234→	if !ok {
   235→		return nil, false, nil
   236→	}
   237→	if optsCopy.Intermediates == nil && len(req.TLS.PeerCertificates) > 1 {
   238→		optsCopy.Intermediates = x509.NewCertPool()
   239→		for _, intermediate := range req.TLS.PeerCertificates[1:] {
   240→			optsCopy.Intermediates.AddCert(intermediate)
   241→		}
   242→	}
   243→
   244→	if _, err := req.TLS.PeerCertificates[0].Verify(optsCopy); err != nil {
   245→		return nil, false, err
   246→	}
   247→	if err := a.verifySubject(req.TLS.PeerCertificates[0].Subject); err != nil {
   248→		return nil, false, err
   249→	}
   250→	return a.auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)
   251→}
   252→
   253→func (a *Verifier) verifySubject(subject pkix.Name) error {
   254→	// No CN restrictions
   255→	if len(a.allowedCommonNames.Value()) == 0 {
   256→		return nil
   257→	}
   258→	// Enforce CN restrictions
   259→	for _, allowedCommonName := range a.allowedCommonNames.Value() {
   260→		if allowedCommonName == subject.CommonName {
   261→			return nil
   262→		}
   263→	}
   264→	return fmt.Errorf("x509: subject with cn=%s is not in the allowed list", subject.CommonName)
   265→}
   266→
   267→// DefaultVerifyOptions returns VerifyOptions that use the system root certificates, current time,
   268→// and requires certificates to be valid for client auth (x509.ExtKeyUsageClientAuth)
   269→func DefaultVerifyOptions() x509.VerifyOptions {
   270→	return x509.VerifyOptions{
   271→		KeyUsages: []x509.ExtKeyUsage{x509.ExtKeyUsageClientAuth},
   272→	}
   273→}
   274→
   275→// CommonNameUserConversion builds user info from a certificate chain using the subject's CommonName
   276→var CommonNameUserConversion = UserConversionFunc(func(chain []*x509.Certificate) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error) {
   277→	if len(chain[0].Subject.CommonName) == 0 {
   278→		return nil, false, nil
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	fp := sha256.Sum256(chain[0].Raw)
   282→	id := "X509SHA256=" + hex.EncodeToString(fp[:])
   283→
   284→	return &authenticator.Response{
   285→		User: &user.DefaultInfo{
   286→			Name:   chain[0].Subject.CommonName,
   287→			Groups: chain[0].Subject.Organization,
   288→			Extra: map[string][]string{
   289→				user.CredentialIDKey: {id},
   290→			},
   291→		},
   292→	}, true, nil
   293→})

    36→func (a *Authenticator) AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error) {
    37→	if len(a.allowedPaths) > 0 && !a.allowedPaths[req.URL.Path] {

    41→	auds, _ := authenticator.AudiencesFrom(req.Context())

    44→			Name:   anonymousUser,
    45→			Groups: []string{unauthenticatedGroup},

    47→		Audiences: auds,

    56→	allowedPaths := make(map[string]bool)

    59→// JWTTokenGenerator returns a TokenGenerator that generates signed JWT tokens, using the given privateKey.
    60→// privateKey is a PEM-encoded byte array of a private RSA key.
    61→func JWTTokenGenerator(iss string, privateKey interface{}) (TokenGenerator, error) {
    62→	var signer jose.Signer
    63→	var err error
    64→	switch pk := privateKey.(type) {
    65→	case *rsa.PrivateKey:
    66→		signer, err = signerFromRSAPrivateKey(pk)
    67→		if err != nil {
    68→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not generate signer for RSA keypair: %v", err)
    69→		}
    70→	case *ecdsa.PrivateKey:
    71→		signer, err = signerFromECDSAPrivateKey(pk)
    72→		if err != nil {
    73→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not generate signer for ECDSA keypair: %v", err)
    74→		}
    75→	case jose.OpaqueSigner:
    76→		signer, err = signerFromOpaqueSigner(pk)
    77→		if err != nil {
    78→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not generate signer for OpaqueSigner: %v", err)
    79→		}
    80→	default:
    81→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown private key type %T, must be *rsa.PrivateKey, *ecdsa.PrivateKey, or jose.OpaqueSigner", privateKey)
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	return &jwtTokenGenerator{
    85→		iss:    iss,
    86→		signer: signer,
    87→	}, nil
    88→}

    90→// keyIDFromPublicKey derives a key ID non-reversibly from a public key.
    91→//
    92→// The Key ID is field on a given on JWTs and JWKs that help relying parties
    93→// pick the correct key for verification when the identity party advertises
    94→// multiple keys.
    95→//
    96→// Making the derivation non-reversible makes it impossible for someone to
    97→// accidentally obtain the real key from the key ID and use it for token
    98→// validation.
    99→func keyIDFromPublicKey(publicKey interface{}) (string, error) {
   100→	publicKeyDERBytes, err := x509.MarshalPKIXPublicKey(publicKey)
   101→	if err != nil {
   102→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to serialize public key to DER format: %v", err)
   103→	}
   104→
   105→	hasher := crypto.SHA256.New()
   106→	hasher.Write(publicKeyDERBytes)
   107→	publicKeyDERHash := hasher.Sum(nil)
   108→
   109→	keyID := base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(publicKeyDERHash)
   110→
   111→	return keyID, nil
   112→}
   113→
   114→func signerFromRSAPrivateKey(keyPair *rsa.PrivateKey) (jose.Signer, error) {
   115→	keyID, err := keyIDFromPublicKey(&keyPair.PublicKey)
   116→	if err != nil {
   117→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to derive keyID: %v", err)
   118→	}
   119→
   120→	// IMPORTANT: If this function is updated to support additional key sizes,
   121→	// algorithmForPublicKey in serviceaccount/openidmetadata.go and
   122→	// validateJWTHeader in externaljwt/pkg/plugin/plugin.go must also
   123→	// be updated to support the same key sizes. Today we only support RS256.
   124→
   125→	// Wrap the RSA keypair in a JOSE JWK with the designated key ID.
   126→	privateJWK := &jose.JSONWebKey{
   127→		Algorithm: string(jose.RS256),
   128→		Key:       keyPair,
   129→		KeyID:     keyID,
   130→		Use:       "sig",
   131→	}
   132→
   133→	signer, err := jose.NewSigner(
   134→		jose.SigningKey{
   135→			Algorithm: jose.RS256,
   136→			Key:       privateJWK,
   137→		},
   138→		nil,
   139→	)
   140→
   141→	if err != nil {
   142→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create signer: %v", err)
   143→	}
   144→
   145→	return signer, nil
   146→}
   147→
   148→func signerFromECDSAPrivateKey(keyPair *ecdsa.PrivateKey) (jose.Signer, error) {
   149→	var alg jose.SignatureAlgorithm
   150→
   151→	// IMPORTANT: If this function is updated to support additional algorithms,
   152→	// validateJWTHeader in externaljwt/pkg/plugin/plugin.go must also be updated
   153→	// to support the same Algorithms. Today we only support "ES256", "ES384", "ES512".
   154→
   155→	switch keyPair.Curve {
   156→	case elliptic.P256():
   157→		alg = jose.ES256
   158→	case elliptic.P384():
   159→		alg = jose.ES384
   160→	case elliptic.P521():
   161→		alg = jose.ES512
   162→	default:
   163→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown private key curve, must be 256, 384, or 521")
   164→	}
   165→
   166→	keyID, err := keyIDFromPublicKey(&keyPair.PublicKey)
   167→	if err != nil {
   168→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to derive keyID: %v", err)
   169→	}
   170→
   171→	// Wrap the ECDSA keypair in a JOSE JWK with the designated key ID.
   172→	privateJWK := &jose.JSONWebKey{
   173→		Algorithm: string(alg),
   174→		Key:       keyPair,
   175→		KeyID:     keyID,
   176→		Use:       "sig",
   177→	}
   178→
   179→	signer, err := jose.NewSigner(
   180→		jose.SigningKey{
   181→			Algorithm: alg,
   182→			Key:       privateJWK,
   183→		},
   184→		nil,
   185→	)
   186→	if err != nil {
   187→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create signer: %v", err)
   188→	}
   189→
   190→	return signer, nil
   191→}

   193→func signerFromOpaqueSigner(opaqueSigner jose.OpaqueSigner) (jose.Signer, error) {
   194→	alg := jose.SignatureAlgorithm(opaqueSigner.Public().Algorithm)
   195→
   196→	signer, err := jose.NewSigner(
   197→		jose.SigningKey{
   198→			Algorithm: alg,
   199→			Key: &jose.JSONWebKey{
   200→				Algorithm: string(alg),
   201→				Key:       opaqueSigner,
   202→				KeyID:     opaqueSigner.Public().KeyID,
   203→				Use:       "sig",
   204→			},
   205→		},
   206→		nil,
   207→	)
   208→	if err != nil {
   209→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create signer: %v", err)
   210→	}
   211→
   212→	return signer, nil
   213→}

   246→// PublicKeysGetter returns public keys for a given key id.
   247→type PublicKeysGetter interface {
   248→	// AddListener is adds a listener to be notified of potential input changes.
   249→	// This is a noop on static providers.
   250→	AddListener(listener Listener)
   251→
   252→	// GetCacheAgeMaxSeconds returns the seconds a call to GetPublicKeys() can be cached for.
   253→	// If the results of GetPublicKeys() can be dynamic, this means a new key must be included in the results
   254→	// for at least this long before it is used to sign new tokens.
   255→	GetCacheAgeMaxSeconds() int
   256→
   257→	// GetPublicKeys returns public keys to use for verifying a token with the given key id.
   258→	// keyIDHint may be empty if the token did not have a kid header, or if all public keys are desired.
   259→	GetPublicKeys(ctx context.Context, keyIDHint string) []PublicKey

   262→type PublicKey struct {
   263→	KeyID                    string
   264→	PublicKey                interface{}
   265→	ExcludeFromOIDCDiscovery bool
   266→}
   267→
   268→type staticPublicKeysGetter struct {
   269→	allPublicKeys  []PublicKey
   270→	publicKeysByID map[string][]PublicKey
   271→}
   272→
   273→// StaticPublicKeysGetter constructs an implementation of PublicKeysGetter
   274→// which returns all public keys when key id is unspecified, and returns
   275→// the public keys matching the keyIDFromPublicKey-derived key id when
   276→// a key id is specified.
   277→func StaticPublicKeysGetter(keys []interface{}) (PublicKeysGetter, error) {
   278→	allPublicKeys := []PublicKey{}
   279→	publicKeysByID := map[string][]PublicKey{}
   280→	for _, key := range keys {
   281→		if privateKey, isPrivateKey := key.(publicKeyGetter); isPrivateKey {
   282→			// This is a private key. Extract its public key.
   283→			key = privateKey.Public()
   284→		}
   285→
   286→		keyID, err := keyIDFromPublicKey(key)
   287→		if err != nil {
   288→			return nil, err
   289→		}
   290→		pk := PublicKey{PublicKey: key, KeyID: keyID}
   291→		publicKeysByID[keyID] = append(publicKeysByID[keyID], pk)
   292→		allPublicKeys = append(allPublicKeys, pk)
   293→	}
   294→	return &staticPublicKeysGetter{
   295→		allPublicKeys:  allPublicKeys,
   296→		publicKeysByID: publicKeysByID,
   297→	}, nil
   298→}

   300→func (s staticPublicKeysGetter) AddListener(listener Listener) {
   301→	// no-op, static key content never changes
   302→}

   304→func (s staticPublicKeysGetter) GetCacheAgeMaxSeconds() int {
   305→	// hard-coded to match cache max-age set in OIDC discovery
   306→	return 3600

   309→func (s staticPublicKeysGetter) GetPublicKeys(ctx context.Context, keyID string) []PublicKey {
   310→	if len(keyID) == 0 {
   311→		return s.allPublicKeys

   313→	return s.publicKeysByID[keyID]

   317→	issuers      map[string]bool

   323→// Validator is called by the JWT token authenticator to apply domain specific
   324→// validation to a token and extract user information.
   325→// PrivateClaims is the struct that the authenticator should deserialize the JWT payload into, thus
   326→// it should contain fields for any private claims that the Validator requires to validate the JWT.
   327→type Validator[PrivateClaims any] interface {
   328→	// Validate validates a token and returns user information or an error.
   329→	// Validator can assume that the issuer and signature of a token are already
   330→	// verified when this function is called.
   331→	Validate(ctx context.Context, tokenData string, public *jwt.Claims, private *PrivateClaims) (*apiserverserviceaccount.ServiceAccountInfo, error)
   332→}
   333→
   334→func (j *jwtTokenAuthenticator[PrivateClaims]) AuthenticateToken(ctx context.Context, tokenData string) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error) {
   335→	if !j.hasCorrectIssuer(tokenData) {
   336→		return nil, false, nil
   337→	}
   338→
   339→	tok, err := jwt.ParseSigned(tokenData)
   340→	if err != nil {
   341→		return nil, false, nil
   342→	}
   343→
   344→	public := &jwt.Claims{}
   345→	private := new(PrivateClaims)
   346→
   347→	// Pick the key that has the same key ID as `tok`, if one exists.
   348→	var kid string
   349→	for _, header := range tok.Headers {
   350→		if header.KeyID != "" {
   351→			kid = header.KeyID
   352→			break
   353→		}
   354→	}
   355→
   356→	var (
   357→		found   bool
   358→		errlist []error
   359→	)
   360→	keys := j.keysGetter.GetPublicKeys(ctx, kid)
   361→	if len(keys) == 0 {
   362→		return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("invalid signature, no keys found")
   363→	}
   364→	for _, key := range keys {
   365→		if err := tok.Claims(key.PublicKey, public, private); err != nil {
   366→			errlist = append(errlist, err)
   367→			continue
   368→		}
   369→		found = true
   370→		break
   371→	}
   372→
   373→	if !found {
   374→		return nil, false, utilerrors.NewAggregate(errlist)
   375→	}
   376→
   377→	// sanity check issuer since we parsed it out before signature validation
   378→	if !j.issuers[public.Issuer] {
   379→		return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("token issuer %q is invalid", public.Issuer)
   380→	}
   381→
   382→	tokenAudiences := authenticator.Audiences(public.Audience)
   383→	if len(tokenAudiences) == 0 {
   384→		// only apiserver audiences are allowed for legacy tokens
   385→		audit.AddAuditAnnotation(ctx, "authentication.k8s.io/legacy-token", public.Subject)
   386→		legacyTokensTotal.WithContext(ctx).Inc()
   387→		tokenAudiences = j.implicitAuds
   388→	}
   389→
   390→	requestedAudiences, ok := authenticator.AudiencesFrom(ctx)
   391→	if !ok {
   392→		// default to apiserver audiences
   393→		requestedAudiences = j.implicitAuds
   394→	}
   395→
   396→	auds := authenticator.Audiences(tokenAudiences).Intersect(requestedAudiences)
   397→	if len(auds) == 0 && len(j.implicitAuds) != 0 {
   398→		return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("token audiences %q is invalid for the target audiences %q", tokenAudiences, requestedAudiences)
   399→	}
   400→
   401→	// If we get here, we have a token with a recognized signature and
   402→	// issuer string.
   403→	sa, err := j.validator.Validate(ctx, tokenData, public, private)
   404→	if err != nil {
   405→		return nil, false, err
   406→	}
   407→
   408→	return &authenticator.Response{
   409→		User:      sa.UserInfo(),
   410→		Audiences: auds,
   411→	}, true, nil
   412→}
   413→
   414→// hasCorrectIssuer returns true if tokenData is a valid JWT in compact
   415→// serialization format and the "iss" claim matches the iss field of this token
   416→// authenticator, and otherwise returns false.
   417→//
   418→// Note: go-jose currently does not allow access to unverified JWS payloads.
   419→// See https://github.com/square/go-jose/issues/169
   420→func (j *jwtTokenAuthenticator[PrivateClaims]) hasCorrectIssuer(tokenData string) bool {
   421→	if strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(tokenData), "{") {
   422→		return false
   423→	}
   424→	parts := strings.Split(tokenData, ".")
   425→	if len(parts) != 3 {
   426→		return false
   427→	}
   428→	payload, err := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[1])
   429→	if err != nil {
   430→		return false
   431→	}
   432→	claims := struct {
